subject: LIGO/Virgo S200114f: No counterpart candidates in Fermi-LAT observations
body:
L. Scotton (CNRS/IN2P3/LUPM), M. Crnogorcevic (Univ. of Maryland & 
NASA/GSFC), M. Axelsson (KTH & Stockholm Univ.) and F. Longo (University 
and INFN, Trieste) report on behalf of the Fermi-LAT Collaboration:

We have searched data collected by the Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT) 
on Jan 14, 2020,ïŋ―ïŋ―for possible high-energy (E > 100 MeV) gamma-ray 
emission inïŋ―ïŋ―spatial/temporalïŋ―ïŋ―coincidence with the LIGO/Virgo trigger 
S200114f (GCN 26734).

We define "instantaneous coverage" as the integral over the region of 
the LIGOïŋ―ïŋ―probability map that is within the LAT field of view at a given 
time, andïŋ―ïŋ―"cumulativeïŋ―ïŋ―coverage" as the integral of the instantaneous 
coverage over time.ïŋ―ïŋ―Fermi-LAT hadïŋ―ïŋ―an instantaneous coverage of ~87% of 
the LIGO probability at theïŋ―ïŋ―time of the triggerïŋ―ïŋ―(T0 = 
2020-01-1402:08:18.230UTC), and reached 100%ïŋ―ïŋ―cumulative coverageïŋ―ïŋ―after 
~4.2 ks.

We performed a search for a transient counterpart within the observed 
region of the 90% contour ofïŋ―ïŋ―LIGO map in a fixed time window from T0 to 
T0 + 10 ks.ïŋ―ïŋ―No significant new sourcesïŋ―ïŋ―are found.

We also performed a search which adapted the time interval of the 
analysis to theïŋ―ïŋ―exposure of each region of the sky, and no additional 
excesses were found.

Energy flux upper bounds for the fixed time intervalïŋ―ïŋ―between 100 MeV and 
1 GeVïŋ―ïŋ―for this search varyïŋ―ïŋ―between 2e-10 and 2e-08 [erg/cm^2/s].

The Fermi-LAT point of contact for this event is Milena Crnogorcevic 
(mcrnogor@astro.umd.edu).

The Fermi-LAT is a pair conversion telescope designed to coverïŋ―ïŋ―the 
energy bandïŋ―ïŋ―from 20 MeV to greater than 300 GeV.ïŋ―ïŋ―It is the product of an 
international collaboration betweenïŋ―ïŋ―NASA and DOE in the U.S. and many 
scientific institutionsïŋ―ïŋ―across France,ïŋ―ïŋ―Italy, Japan and Sweden.
